zotero.abstractNote: Generalized linear models (GLMs) are a cornerstone of statistical modeling. However, their training via iterative optimization, such as Newton's method, can be computationally expensive for large datasets. We propose a method to accelerate the training of GLMs by trading off computational effort for uncertainty. Specifically, we replace the exact Hessian with a stochastic approximation that is cheaper to compute, while maintaining a probabilistic interpretation of the model parameters. We show that this approach leads to significant speedups in training time while providing well-calibrated uncertainty estimates. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on several real-world datasets.
